title: Build a fine-tuning evaluation harness first
description: Stand up task, capability, and safety evals before training so every checkpoint is judged on fixed, comparable criteria.
body: Use before launching any fine-tuning run. Do not start training without a frozen eval — you cannot tell improvement from regression or compare checkpoints. Actions: assemble a held-out task eval, a general-capability holdout, and a safety/refusal set; score the base first as a baseline, then score each checkpoint identically. Validation signals include a baseline score for the base model and per-checkpoint scores on all three eval sets. Risk boundary: training blind ships regressions; if the capability or safety holdout cannot be assembled, escalate before any model is promoted to serving.
